en.extract:
The chestnut-and-black weaver (Ploceus castaneofuscus) is a species of bird in the family Ploceidae. It  is found in West Africa from Sierra Leone to southern Nigeria.
The chestnut-and-black weaver was formerly treated as a subspecies of Vieillot's black weaver (Ploceus nigerrimus). The species were split based on the striking differences in the colour of the plumage.

The '''chestnut-and-black weaver''' ('''''Ploceus castaneofuscus''''') is a species of bird in the family [[Ploceidae]]. It  is found in West Africa from Sierra Leone to southern Nigeria.

The chestnut-and-black weaver was formerly treated as a [[subspecies]] of [[Vieillot's black weaver]] (''Ploceus nigerrimus''). The species were split based on the striking differences in the colour of the plumage.<ref name=ioc>{{cite web| editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor1-link=Frank Gill (ornithologist) | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | editor3-last=Rasmussen | editor3-first=Pamela | editor3-link=Pamela Rasmussen | date=January 2023 | title=Old World sparrows, snowfinches, weavers | work=IOC World Bird List Version 13.1 | url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/weavers/ | publisher=International Ornithologists' Union | access-date=7 February 2023 }}</ref><ref name=bow>{{cite journal | last1=Craig | first1=A.J.F. | last2=del Hoyo | first2=J. |  last3=Collar | first3=N. | last4=Kirwan | first4=G.M. | date=2022 | title=Chestnut-and-black Weaver (''Ploceus castaneofuscus''), version 1.0 | editor1-last=Billerman | editor1-first=S.M. | editor2-last=Sly | editor2-first=N.D. | journal=Birds of the World | location=Ithaca, NY, USA | publisher=Cornell Lab of Ornithology | doi=10.2173/bow.viewea2.01 }}</ref>
